behavior_surfaces: workflow template authoring guide model/skills guidance
workflow runtime recommended skill attention-only semantics
state_contracts: phases[].skills are auxiliary attention metadata only; they do not define workflow flow, phase order, transition authority, completion criteria, tool permissions, or SkillTool auto-execution.
Workflow control flow is owned by ordered phases, transitions, handoff rules, completion criteria, and runtime/tool policy, not by recommended skill names.
Author-created phase recommended skill references must come from workflow_template_authoring skill_catalog or from skill_create's returned recommendedReference.
When no installed or newly created skill fits a phase, authoring guidance should omit phases[].skills instead of inventing or mirroring workflow step names.
Create/update authoring paths reject unavailable recommended phase skills before persistence; imported or legacy unresolved references may be preserved as visible dependency diagnostics.
